basic_details.job_title: GMCH Chandigarh Recruitment 2026 – Walk in for 64 Senior Residents, CMO and More Posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: Senior Residents, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, Resident Pathologist
basic_details.short_information: Government Medical College & Hospital (GMCH), Chandigarh has invited applications from Indian Nationals for filling 64 tenure posts of Senior Residents, Casualty Medical Officers (CMO) in General Medicine and General Surgery, Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist. Selection will be through a walk-in interview scheduled on 20 July 2026 (Monday). These are tenure posts, and appointments will be made for a period of 44 days. The notice is issued by the Establishment Branch-II, GMCH, Chandigarh, dated 15 July 2026.

application_fee.details: Non-refundable fee of ₹500/-, deposited on the spot at GMCH Applicants applying for multiple posts/departments need to deposit this fee only once The application fee, once deposited, will not be refunded under any circumstances

age_limit.details: Upper age limit as on 1 July 2026: 45 years, applicable for Senior Residents, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist.

qualification.details: Only Indian Nationals are allowed to apply. Candidates of foreign nationality are not eligible for these posts. | For all four post categories - Senior Resident, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist - the essential qualification is: | A Postgraduate Medical Degree, i.e., M.D./M.S./DNB/M.Ch./D.M. or equivalent in the concerned specialty, as per the Central Residency Scheme The degree must be recognized by the Medical Council of India (MCI) and the candidate registered with either the Medical Council of India or a State Medical Council Candidates who have already completed a three-year tenure of Senior Residency are not eligible for the post of Senior Resident

salary_details.details: Senior Residents, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist holding a PG degree will be paid in the pay scale of ₹67,700/- (minimum pay, 1st Cell of Level-11, as per Schedule Part A of the Pay Matrix), plus Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) at 20%, as per the Central Government Services (Revised Pay) Rules, 2016.

selection_process: Selection is through walk-in interview only. There is no mention of a written examination in this notice.
how_to_apply: Download the prescribed application form from the official website of GMCH
Fill the form completely in the prescribed format
Attach self-attested copies of all required certificates, mark sheets, registration proof, NOC (if applicable), and a recent passport-size photograph
Report to the Establishment Branch-II, Block-D, GMCH-32, Chandigarh, on 20 July 2026, between 9:00 A.M. and 11:00 A.M.
Submit the filled application form along with the non-refundable application fee of ₹500/- and photocopies of testimonials, before 11:00 A.M.
Appear for the walk-in interview at 2:00 P.M. the same day, in Room No. 210, Level-2, Block-D, GMCH, Chandigarh

rows.details: Senior Residents, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, Resident Pathologist

rows.date_time: Office of Director Principal, Room No. 210, Level-2, Block-D, GMCH, Chandigarh

content_sections.heading: GMCH Vacancy Details: Department-wise
content_sections.text: The total across all four post categories is 64 , matching the sum stated in the source notification.

content_sections.heading: GMCH Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Only Indian Nationals are allowed to apply. Candidates of foreign nationality are not eligible for these posts. For all four post categories - Senior Resident, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist - the essential qualification is: A Postgraduate Medical Degree, i.e., M.D./M.S./DNB/M.Ch./D.M. or equivalent in the concerned specialty, as per the Central Residency Scheme The degree must be recognized by the Medical Council of India (MCI) and the candidate registered with either the Medical Council of India or a State Medical Council Candidates who have already completed a three-year tenure of Senior Residency are not eligible for the post of Senior Resident

content_sections.heading: GMCH Age Limit
content_sections.text: Upper age limit as on 1 July 2026: 45 years , applicable for Senior Residents, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist.

rows.category: Widows, deserted women, and women judicially separated from their husbands (not remarried)

content_sections.heading: GMCH Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Senior Residents, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist holding a PG degree will be paid in the pay scale of ₹67,700/- (minimum pay, 1st Cell of Level-11, as per Schedule Part A of the Pay Matrix), plus Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) at 20%, as per the Central Government Services (Revised Pay) Rules, 2016.

content_sections.heading: GMCH Application Fee
content_sections.text: Non-refundable fee of ₹500/-, deposited on the spot at GMCH Applicants applying for multiple posts/departments need to deposit this fee only once The application fee, once deposited, will not be refunded under any circumstances

content_sections.heading: GMCH Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is through walk-in interview only. There is no mention of a written examination in this notice.

content_sections.heading: Tenure of the Posts
content_sections.text: The posts are tenure posts; appointments will be made for a period of 44 days If work and conduct are not found satisfactory at any stage, services may be terminated without assigning any reason If Senior Residency is done at any Central Institution/Hospital under the Central Residency Scheme, that period is counted toward the maximum tenure of three years (including previous experience); the maximum period of Senior Residency cannot exceed three years

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Application form in the prescribed format, duly filled Self-attested copies of educational qualifications and experience certificates Date of birth certificate Registration certificate with MCI/State Medical Council Internship completion certificate Mark sheets of MBBS 1st Prof, 2nd Prof, and Final Prof-Part I & II No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the present employer, for candidates already in Government service (see Important Instructions) Recent passport-size photograph Photocopies of relevant certificates/testimonials, plus original certificates/testimonials for verification on the day

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for GMCH Walk-in Interview 2026
content_sections.text: Download the prescribed application form from the official website of GMCH Fill the form completely in the prescribed format Attach self-attested copies of all required certificates, mark sheets, registration proof, NOC (if applicable), and a recent passport-size photograph Report to the Establishment Branch-II, Block-D, GMCH-32, Chandigarh, on 20 July 2026, between 9:00 A.M. and 11:00 A.M. Submit the filled application form along with the non-refundable application fee of ₹500/- and photocopies of testimonials, before 11:00 A.M. Carry original certificates/testimonials for verification Appear for the walk-in interview at 2:00 P.M. the same day, in Room No. 210, Level-2, Block-D, GMCH, Chandigarh

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in the GMCH Chandigarh walk-in interview 2026?
faq.answer: There are 64 vacancies across Senior Resident, CMO (General Medicine & General Surgery), Resident Anaesthetist, and Resident Pathologist posts.

faq.question: Q2. When is the GMCH Chandigarh walk-in interview?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled for 20 July 2026 (Monday) at 2:00 P.M.

faq.question: Q3. What is the qualification required for these posts?
faq.answer: A Postgraduate Medical Degree (M.D./M.S./DNB/M.Ch./D.M.) or equivalent in the concerned specialty, recognized by MCI, with registration with MCI or a State Medical Council.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for GMCH walk-in interview 2026?
faq.answer: ₹500/-, non-refundable, deposited on the spot at GMCH. It needs to be paid only once even if applying for multiple posts.

faq.question: Q5. What is the pay scale for these posts?
faq.answer: ₹67,700/- (minimum pay, 1st Cell of Level-11) plus NPA at 20%, as per the Central Government Services (Revised Pay) Rules, 2016.

faq.question: Q6. What is the tenure of these posts?
faq.answer: These are tenure posts, and appointments will be made for a period of 44 days.

faq.question: Q7. What is the age limit for GMCH Senior Resident and CMO posts?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 45 years as on 1 July 2026, with a 10-year relaxation for widows, deserted women, and women judicially separated from their husbands (not remarried).

faq.question: Q8. Can candidates already in government service apply?
faq.answer: Yes, but they must route their application through proper channel and attach an NOC, or a letter requesting an NOC, from their present employer; otherwise the application will not be entertained.
